jokes.text: This punishment is not medieval.
jokes.setup: Chalkboard gag - Bart writing lines as punishment
jokes.punchline: Claiming his punishment isn't medieval while doing the most medieval punishment of all
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons self-aware humor with Bart's perfect obliviousness to irony

jokes.text: Oh, I like creating disappointment. You know that little moment where people's hope dies? I feed on that.
jokes.setup: People asking if he's leaving a parking space
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy reveals he enjoys psychological cruelty for its own sake
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy characterization - turning a mundane parking situation into existential cruelty

jokes.text: We listened to music radio in the car.
jokes.setup: Kids complaining about being tricked into going to a boring mall trip
jokes.punchline: Lisa considers listening to music radio a significant sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Subtle character beat showing Lisa's pretentiousness about popular music

jokes.text: I want to go lecture the food court businesses that are still using Styrofoam.
jokes.setup: Kids get to pick one fun thing to do at the mall
jokes.punchline: Lisa's idea of fun is environmental activism
jokes.explanation: Classic Lisa characterization - her activism is so ingrained she can't separate it from fun

jokes.text: Lead-based toys! Lead-based toys! Sit on a bench! Sit on a bench!
jokes.setup: Kids shouting their preferred mall activities
jokes.punchline: Bart wants dangerous toys, baby Maggie just wants to sit
jokes.explanation: Absurd contrast between Bart's chaos and Maggie's simple desires

jokes.text: Like what? - Food. - Pass. - Shelter? - That dump?! - Clothing? - I wouldn't blow my nose into this.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming kids don't remember nice things parents do
jokes.punchline: Bart systematically rejecting all basic necessities Homer provides
jokes.explanation: Well-structured escalation with Bart's ungrateful responses getting increasingly harsh

jokes.text: Video's broken, Alan! Call Eric! I'm Eric! Black Eric!
jokes.setup: Employee trying to call for help with broken video
jokes.punchline: Confusion about which Eric, resolved by racial identifier
jokes.explanation: Minor workplace confusion gag, competent but unremarkable

jokes.text: I want a poodle. I'd stay away from poodles. They give the dog... ideas.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to stuff a poodle
jokes.punchline: Mysterious warning about poodles giving dogs 'ideas'
jokes.explanation: Mysteriously ominous warning creates funny ambiguity about what poodle 'ideas' could mean

jokes.text: Doctor. - Okay... nurse it is. Professor. - Kindergarten teacher. - Chef! - Lunch lady! - CEO! Secretary to a CEO.
jokes.setup: Employee asking what outfit Maggie wants for her dolphin
jokes.punchline: Employee keeps downgrading Maggie's ambitious career choices
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing sexist assumptions about career limitations

jokes.text: We fooled her... Betsy.
jokes.setup: Successfully tricking the store employee
jokes.punchline: Bart's awkward pause before adding the employee's name
jokes.explanation: Minor timing gag with Bart's unnecessary name addition

jokes.text: Wow, this guy could put Milhouse out of business.
jokes.setup: Seeing a nerdy stuffed animal
jokes.punchline: Bart treating nerdiness like a competitive business
jokes.explanation: Clever take on Milhouse's nerdiness as his defining professional characteristic

jokes.text: Homer is a lard butt... Homer has booger breath... Homer is a big jerk... Dung muncher. Stink brains. Cheese wang. Captain Crud... B.O. Baracus. Mount Baldy. Tub of Guts. Crapper Clogger.
jokes.setup: Cute stuffed animal starts insulting Homer
jokes.punchline: Increasingly creative and elaborate insults from innocent toy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of insults with creative terminology, though somewhat juvenile

jokes.text: Dung muncher. Stink brains. Cheese wang. Captain Crud. B.O. Baracus. Mount Baldy. Tub of Guts. Crapper Clogger.
jokes.setup: Dolphin continues insulting Homer
jokes.punchline: Increasingly creative and absurd insults, especially 'B.O. Baracus'
jokes.explanation: 'B.O. Baracus' is a particularly clever A-Team reference that elevates the list

jokes.text: I'll kill you all!
jokes.setup: Homer getting increasingly angry at insulting toy
jokes.punchline: Homer's rage reaches murderous levels over toy insults
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer rage escalation, competent but predictable

jokes.text: Uh-oh, that's where the food sleeps.
jokes.setup: Hearing noises from the kitchen during suspected break-in
jokes.punchline: Homer's childlike personification of food storage
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Homer line - shows his food obsession and childlike worldview in one absurd phrase

jokes.text: Why would a robber make pie? Who cares? If you kill him, we'll be on TV!
jokes.setup: Homer questioning why a robber would bake
jokes.punchline: Bart's bloodthirsty excitement about television fame
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Bart's warped priorities and media obsession

jokes.text: You're just like that show Scrubs.
jokes.setup: Complaining about Grandma's appearing and disappearing
jokes.punchline: Comparing family drama to a TV show known for fantasy sequences
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference with decent setup but somewhat cheap payoff

jokes.text: You already ate the whole thing. Well, I'm not going to digest it!
jokes.setup: Homer refusing pie to prove his point
jokes.punchline: Homer's impossible threat to not digest food he already ate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer absurdity - trying to rebel against biology itself

jokes.text: When did he lose his cuteness? Oh, I have a picture of that right here.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking when baby Homer lost his cuteness
jokes.punchline: Mona having a specific photo documenting the exact moment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant concept - the idea of documenting the exact moment cuteness died is both absurd and relatable

jokes.text: But at least he wasn't raised by a television.
jokes.setup: Mona feeling bad about missing Homer's childhood
jokes.punchline: Cut to Homer literally being raised by TV while she's away protesting
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic cut showing exactly what she claims didn't happen

jokes.text: You left a hole in heart that could never be filled. So I filled it with food. But I'm never full.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his emotional damage from abandonment
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of metaphorical hole, leading to endless eating
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment explaining Homer's eating habits through genuine emotional pain

jokes.text: Hey, Lance, nice trousers. Did you win those at the Tour de Pants? Wokka, wokka!... Yeah, well, I never thought you were funny. Ever.
jokes.setup: Fozzie making his signature bad pun at awards show
jokes.punchline: Lance Armstrong's brutal, public rejection of Fozzie's comedy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy - Lance's harsh honesty about Fozzie's beloved terrible puns

jokes.text: I'm still with you, son. Oh, this just gets worse and worse!
jokes.setup: Homer discovering his mother is dead
jokes.punchline: Her ghost appearing makes the situation even worse for Homer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's horror at supernatural complications

jokes.text: First, it was gonna be the Hustle. Recently, I've been taking tap lessons. But now? Well, I don't feel much like dancing.
jokes.setup: Grampa planning to dance on Mona's grave
jokes.punchline: His elaborate dance preparation undermined by actual grief
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Grampa's petty planning contrasted with genuine emotion

jokes.text: And I'd say you suck. And she would say you don't suck that bad. She said I didn't suck? That bad.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to comfort Homer about what Grandma said
jokes.punchline: Homer focusing on the backhanded nature of the compliment
jokes.explanation: Nice character beat showing Homer's desperate need for validation even when qualified

jokes.text: Aw, I wish I was your son, too... Uh, Moe, Lenny's mom says she loves you.
jokes.setup: Lenny calling his mom and praising her cookies to Moe
jokes.punchline: Lenny's mom expressing more affection for Moe than her own son
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing the weird dynamics of the bar regulars

jokes.text: Manjula will sell the store, dye her hair blonde, and marry my cousin Jangelvar. Yes, I will.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Apu what happens after death
jokes.punchline: Apu's specific prediction of his wife's post-death behavior, confirmed by her
jokes.explanation: Good marital humor with Manjula's matter-of-fact confirmation of Apu's fears

jokes.text: That tiny mouse in the nacho cheese. Oh, for crying out loud, people aren't mice.
jokes.setup: Apu suggesting reincarnation possibilities
jokes.punchline: Pointing to mouse in concession stand food, Ned's literal-minded response
jokes.explanation: Decent visual gag setup with Ned's characteristically literal interpretation

jokes.text: Look, Homer, people don't come back as anything, except for our Lord, who came back as bread.
jokes.setup: Ned dismissing reincarnation beliefs
jokes.punchline: Christian communion bread as literal resurrection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned line showing his literal biblical interpretation in absurd way

jokes.text: That's the thing with your religion: it's a bummer. Even the sing-alongs? No, the sing-alongs are okay.
jokes.setup: Homer criticizing Christianity as depressing
jokes.punchline: Homer's specific exemption for church music
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Homer's selective religious critique

jokes.text: That's the thing with your religion: it's a bummer. Even the sing-alongs? No, the sing-alongs are okay.
jokes.setup: Homer criticizing Ned's religion
jokes.punchline: Making exception for the musical aspects
jokes.explanation: Decent Homer moment showing his selective appreciation for religion.

jokes.text: A donut from the future?
jokes.setup: Marge showing Homer a DVD from his mother's things
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion about DVD technology mixed with donut obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer line combining technological confusion with food obsession

jokes.text: Remember, he brought his girlfriend and they got in a fight?
jokes.setup: Identifying video producer Garry
jokes.punchline: Remembering him for unprofessional personal drama at event
jokes.explanation: Minor observational humor about unprofessional service providers

jokes.text: If you are watching this right now, I am dead. Or if I'm not dead, you've gotten into my stuff. But if I am dead, this is my will. And if I'm not, get out of my stuff.
jokes.setup: Standard video will opening
jokes.punchline: Paranoid about people accessing her belongings while alive
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific paranoia fitting Mona's fugitive lifestyle

jokes.text: You got nothing, you got nothing.
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa receiving meaningful gifts in the will
jokes.punchline: Bart taunting Maggie for not getting anything
jokes.explanation: Pure Bart moment - finding opportunity to torment even in solemn situations

jokes.text: Hmm, it smells like concerts.
jokes.setup: Receiving hemp purse from hippie mother-in-law
jokes.punchline: Marge's polite way of describing marijuana smell
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge euphemism showing her sheltered perspective on counterculture

jokes.text: Oh, it works on so many levels.
jokes.setup: Watching animal bloopers video
jokes.punchline: Homer applying pretentious analysis to simple slapstick
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-joke about over-analyzing simple comedy, though somewhat obvious

jokes.text: I will avenge you. It's not really avenging her, Dad, just the fulfillment of her last wish. I'm really glad you corrected me, Lisa. People are always really glad when they're corrected.
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically vowing revenge, Lisa's technical correction
jokes.punchline: Homer's sarcastic response to Lisa's pedantry
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic showing Lisa's know-it-all tendency and Homer's reaction

jokes.text: You know, spiritually speaking, Grandma's ashes won't know where they've been thrown from, so... in a way... I'm lying down now.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to rationalize not climbing the monument
jokes.punchline: Using pseudo-spiritual logic to justify laziness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa using her intelligence to justify avoiding physical effort

jokes.text: Hmm, to spare, eh? Marge, your back is so sweaty. I'm starting to slip off.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to have energy to spare while being carried by Marge
jokes.punchline: Maggie commenting on the practical consequences of exertion
jokes.explanation: Minor physical comedy gag with decent observational element

jokes.text: Mom. You used me to pull one more stupid hippie protest. Didn't you?
jokes.setup: Homer realizing his mother's ashes stopped a missile launch
jokes.punchline: Homer's bitter recognition of being manipulated even after death
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Homer's realization about his mother's posthumous activism

jokes.text: Ugh, ever since you started watching Monk, you think you are Monk. You're not Monk.
jokes.setup: Henchman making detailed deductions
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony's annoyed dismissal of TV-inspired detective work
jokes.explanation: Decent meta-humor about TV influence with good Fat Tony characterization

jokes.text: Hey, I've seen your wife. She's no Mamie Van Doren.
jokes.setup: Smithers pointing out Burns is talking to ashes
jokes.punchline: Burns' old-fashioned insult comparing Smithers' wife to 1950s actress
jokes.explanation: Good Burns characterization with period-appropriate cultural reference

jokes.text: On another subject, next Thursday I have to leave work early. I assume that's cool.
jokes.setup: Burns just revealed evil plan to destroy rainforest
jokes.punchline: Burns casually asking for time off in the middle of villain monologue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of villain trope with mundane workplace concern interrupting evil plot

jokes.text: My purse is made of hemp. If we burn it, the silly smoke will mellow out those guards.
jokes.setup: Need to get past guards
jokes.punchline: Marge's innocent plan to drug guards with marijuana smoke
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier hemp purse joke with Marge's naive drug use

jokes.text: Hey, did you ever think about how, like, your fingers are like legs for your hand? Imagine what cute little shoes they'd wear. That's already a thing - thimbles, right?
jokes.setup: Guards getting high from hemp smoke
jokes.punchline: Stoned philosophical observation about finger anatomy and thimbles
jokes.explanation: Decent stoned humor with pseudo-philosophical finger observation

jokes.text: Actually, you only hired ten. A couple guys called in sick and the Jewish guy has to leave at sunset.
jokes.setup: Burns asking for reinforcements
jokes.punchline: Smithers' matter-of-fact explanation of staffing issues including religious observance
jokes.explanation: Decent workplace humor mixing evil plotting with HR realities

jokes.text: Now I'll just add water to these ashes and bring my mother back to life. It doesn't work that way, Homie.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to deal with loss of his mother
jokes.punchline: Homer's childlike misunderstanding of death and resurrection
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment showing his magical thinking about death mixed with grief

jokes.text: More sugar, more sugar. - No. - Why not? Because you're sweet enough.
jokes.setup: Homer demanding more sugar
jokes.punchline: Marge's sweet way of saying he's had enough
jokes.explanation: Sweet ending moment showing Marge's loving way of managing Homer
